channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: NACZ Urges Promoters to Delay South African Shows Amid Rising Xenophobic Attacks
item.description: NACZ Urges Promoters to Delay South African Shows Amid Rising Xenophobic Attacks The National Arts Council of Zimbabwe (NACZ) has appealed to local event promoters to consider postponing performances by South African musicians, citing growing public tensions stemming from reported xenophobic attacks in South Africa. The council says the recommendation is a preventive measure designed [...] The post NACZ Urges Promoters to Delay South African Shows Amid Rising Xenophobic Attacks appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Mt Pleasant Homeless Mother’s Desperate Plea: Multiple Harare men want to have lula lula with me and my 3 daughters
item.description: Harare – A harrowing tale of desperation and vulnerability unfolds in Mt Pleasant, Harare, where a homeless mother, Nyarai Dimingo, 39, is battling to protect her three young daughters from the clutches of sex predators. Her family’s plight, brought to light after seven days of living in the open, underscores the escalating crisis of homelessness [...] The post Mt Pleasant Homeless Mother’s Desperate Plea: Multiple Harare men want to have lula lula with me and my 3 daughters first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Pageant empowers people with albinism to stand tall in Zimbabwe
item.description: Xinhua Warm applause echoed through the hall on Saturday night as contestants confidently took to the runway for the Mr and Miss Albinism Zimbabwe pageant. Held in the capital city of Harare, the vibrant event served as a powerful celebration of identity, talent, and resilience. Organized by the Albinism Dare to Dream Initiative (ADDI) to [...] The post Pageant empowers people with albinism to stand tall in Zimbabwe appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Mnangagwa seeks Zulu, Xhosa leaders’ intervention as Zimbabweans fear xenophobic attacks in South Africa
item.description: Mnangagwa seeks Zulu, Xhosa leaders’ intervention as Zimbabweans fear xenophobic attacks in South Africa — via NewZimbabwe.com #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: NewZimbabwe.com

item.title: Another Warriors footballer and Sundowns defender Divine Lunga shot at in R34,000 movie-style ‘cash heist’
item.description: Zimbabwean Footballers Face Peril in South Africa: The Divine Lunga Shooting and a Troubling Pattern Johannesburg – The glitz and glamour often associated with professional football can quickly fade into a stark reality of danger, particularly for Zimbabwean players plying their trade in South Africa. The recent harrowing experience of Divine Lunga, the esteemed defender [...] The post Another Warriors footballer and Sundowns defender Divine Lunga shot at in R34,000 movie-style ‘cash heist’ first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: ZESA Breaks Silence On Fault Behind Zimbabwe’s Nationwide Blackout On Monday Night @ZESAOfficial
item.description: Zimbabwe Went Dark On Monday Night — ZESA Now Explains What Happened ZESA Holdings has finally explained the nationwide blackout that plunged Zimbabwe into darkness on Monday night, 6 July 2026. The power utility said a major electrical fault triggered the collapse, cutting regional interconnections before local electricity generation was also lost. The blackout hit [...] The post ZESA Breaks Silence On Fault Behind Zimbabwe’s Nationwide Blackout On Monday Night appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: PSC rolls out job evaluation reforms to promote fairness & transparency across Public Service @zimsituation
item.description: Source: PSC rolls out job evaluation reforms to promote fairness & transparency across Public Service – herald The Public Service Commission (PSC),working under the auspices of itsparent ministry, is implementing the outcomes of the Job Evaluation (JE)— an exercise which began in and has ongoing resultant actions. In this inclusive and responsive process, the PSC [...] The post PSC rolls out job evaluation reforms to promote fairness & transparency across Public Service appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Mnangagwa appeals for more donations as Zimbabwe struggles to repatriate citizens from South Africa
item.description: President Emmerson Mnangagwa has appealed to the private sector and other well-wishers to provide additional financial support for Zimbabwe’s ongoing repatriation programme to bring home citizens stranded in South Africa. The appeal comes as hundreds of Zimbabweans continue seeking to leave South Africa following weeks of anti-immigrant protests, harassment and attacks targeting foreign nationals. Anti-immigrant [...] The post Mnangagwa appeals for more donations as Zimbabwe struggles to repatriate citizens from South Africa appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe confirms 60 000 returnees as SA migrant exodus grows
item.description: The Citizen The Zimbabwean government has confirmed that more than 60 000 of its citizens have already returned home as anti-illegal immigration sentiments in South Africa forced thousands of Africans to leave the country and protesters took to the streets last Tuesday to reiterate the unofficial deadline for departure. Official figures showed 11 065 Zimbabweans [...] The post Zimbabwe confirms 60 000 returnees as SA migrant exodus grows appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: It feels like I have lost my future, I thought of dying – Zimbabwean siblings say after being chased out of South Africa
item.description: LORRAINE Ncube (18), one of two Zimbabwean siblings being deported from South Africa in ongoing clampdown on illegal migration, says she feels like her future has been lost. Her brother, Lawrence (16), told South African broadcaster SABC News that he thought of dying when they were rounded up from their then Moletji home, outside Polokwane, [...] The post It feels like I have lost my future, I thought of dying – Zimbabwean siblings say after being chased out of South Africa appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: What will be the Consequences for Zimbabwe when CAB3 Passes?
item.description: President Emmerson Mnangagawa has summoned the National Assembly for an extraordinary sitting on 30 th June to consider amendments proposed by the Senate to the Constitutional Amendment Bill No.3. This follows the passage of the Bill on Wednesday through the Senate, 74 in favour and 4 against. Before the Bill can be presented to the [...] The post What will be the Consequences for Zimbabwe when CAB3 Passes? appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: England beat Mexico to reach World Cup quarterfinals
item.description: MEXICO CITY,- England’s national football team defeated Mexico 3-2 in a round-of-16 match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup in Mexico City. England’s goals were scored by Jude Bellingham in the 36th and 38th minutes and Harry Kane in the 60th minute from the penalty spot. Mexico’s goals came from Julian Quinones in the 42nd [...] The post England beat Mexico to reach World Cup quarterfinals appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Nationwide blackout plunges Zimbabwe into darkness as ZESA scrambles to restore power @ZESAOfficial
item.description: Zimbabwe was plunged into darkness on Monday evening after a technical fault on the national electricity grid triggered a nationwide power outage. Power utility ZESA Holdings said the blackout occurred at 6:24pm, affecting the entire country. “ZESA Holdings wishes to inform its valued stakeholders of a nationwide power outage that occurred today, Monday 6 July [...] The post Nationwide blackout plunges Zimbabwe into darkness as ZESA scrambles to restore power appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Has South Africa forgotten Steve Biko? The inferiority complex behind rising Afrophobia @nehandaradio
item.description: Drawing on childhood memories of Zimbabwe hosting liberation refugees, Takura Zhangazha argues that today’s anti-African sentiment in South Africa contradicts the values of Steve Biko, Nelson Mandela and the continent’s liberation struggle. The post Has South Africa forgotten Steve Biko? The inferiority complex behind rising Afrophobia appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Warriors Defender Lunga Escapes Unhurt After Gun Attack in Johannesburg @263Chat
item.description: Zimbabwe international and Mamelodi Sundowns defender Divine Lunga escaped unharmed after gunmen opened fire on his vehicle in Johannesburg prompting South African police to launch an attempted murder investigation. The 30-year-old defender was driving a blue BMW along Tudhope Street in Hillbrow on Sunday with [...] The post Warriors Defender Lunga Escapes Unhurt After Gun Attack in Johannesburg appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Makhadzi urges African unity as Zimbabweans call for boycott of South African artists @nehandaradio
item.description: Makhadzi has appealed for African unity after Zimbabweans called for a boycott of South African artists amid rising tensions over Afrophobia and anti-immigrant violence. The post Makhadzi urges African unity as Zimbabweans call for boycott of South African artists appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio
